title: Software Developer Engineering Tools (M/F/D)*
description:
As a Software Developer for Engineering Tools, you will work on key processes, tools and services that integrate our engineering tools for the design and implementation of intralogistics systems from a technical perspective, thereby delivering lasting improvements to the overall process. You will automate, integrate and optimise – making a tangible contribution to the digitalisation of TGW.

In doing so, you will be involved in the entire lifecycle of our software solutions: from the initial idea through design and development to operation, support and further development.

What you'll be handling:

You will work on the specification, development, maintenance, testing and support of processes, software and services for the TGW Production Engineering Tool Chain. Your focus will be on replacing manual processes with automated, integrated solutions and reducing risks throughout the entire plant lifecycle.

Your responsibilities will include:

- Creating functional and technical specifications
- Conceptualising, designing and implementing software architectures and GUIs
- Developing the necessary software and services
- Conducting unit tests and working according to test-driven development principles
- Testing, commissioning and validating prototypes
- Provisioning the software, including deployment and DevOps activities
- Training and supporting users (2nd level), as well as ongoing maintenance and further development of existing solutions and documentation

What you'll need:

- A completed technical qualification (HTL, FH or university) with a focus on IT
- Excellent knowledge of C#.NET and Microsoft Visual Studio, as well as experience with Docker and Azure DevOps
- Experience with Python is an advantage
- Practical experience in test-driven development and unit testing
- Expertise in working with relational databases; experience with graph databases is an advantage
- Excellent command of German and English
- A self-motivated, service-oriented individual with an entrepreneurial mindset and a passion for automation

What you'll receive:

- Participation in an agile development team, latitude, clear objectives and regular feedback
- Flexible working hours and a generous home office model
- Flat hierarchies and short decision-making processes
- A reliable and stable employer based on the philosophy "Focusing on People – Learning and Growing"

We offer an attractive salary in line with market rates, depending on qualifications and professional experience.

The minimum yearly gross basic salary under the collective agreement, based on full-time employment, is 54,500 Euros.

*TGW is an employer that promotes equal opportunities. We invite everybody interested in this job to apply.
